Rationale for Intervention Flag: March 2010 announcement of new quota formula; sharp immediate quota cut.
Second wave of panic bidding due to announced quota shrinkage for 2011.
High prices due to natural supply scarcity, no exogenous policy shock.
Introduction of MAS loan curbs (causes price drop, not surge).
Market cooling due to LTV limits taking full effect.
Sept 9 announcement of 130bhp power cap for Cat A; panic buying begins.
Sustained panic buying of luxury models to beat the Feb 2014 deadline.
Market begins adjusting prior to actual implementation; inventory clears.
Final month before reclassification; panic has largely subsided.
Implementation of 130bhp cap; luxury cars successfully purged from Cat A.
Cat D motorcycle specific panic (Euro III), but passenger car prices reflect organic demand.
May 27 announcement of MAS loan curb easing (Too late in month to affect May bids).
Massive liquidity injection; buyers utilize new 70% LTV, 7-year loans causing huge demand spike.
Post-shock stabilization; initial pent-up demand satisfied.
Oct 23 announcement of 0% absolute vehicle growth rate sparks FOMO panic bidding.
Post-shock stabilization as market realizes actual supply remains stable via deregistrations.
EV MPO threshold raised to 110kW; mass market EVs suddenly enter Cat A.
Surging demand momentum solidifies as dealerships aggressively pivot to Cat A EVs (e.g., BYD).
July announcement of new 2-quarter rolling average quota calculation severely restricts supply outlook.
Market assimilates new quota reality; new baseline established.
September announcement of severe VES/EEAI rebate cuts effective Jan 1, 2024.
Absolute peak panic buying to physically lock in EV rebates before the looming 2024 deadline.
LTA successfully intervenes with emergency "Cut and Fill" supply injection to break the panic.
Post-rebate cut reality sets in; demand slumps sharply as future demand was pulled into 2023.
Market attempts to establish a new baseline.
Sept 8 announcement of further drastic VES/EEAI cuts effective Jan 1, 2026; immediate surge.
Extreme panic bidding. Dealers rationally pass the impending $10k rebate loss directly into their current COE bids to secure registrations.
Post-shock exhaustion; early adopters have secured their vehicles, demand sharply drops.
Post-rebate cut implementation; the new, permanent market equilibrium is mathematically established.
